title: Add agent-scoped background shell cleanup on agent exit (parity with CC killShellTasksForAgent)
description:
## Gap

CC's killShellTasksForAgent (tasks/LocalShellTask/killShellTasks.ts:53-72) kills all running background bash tasks owned by a specific agent when that agent exits. This prevents background processes from outliving their parent agent (zombie prevention).

OC has no agent ownership model for background shells. BackgroundShellManager (src/tools/bash/mod.rs:38-268) tracks shells by shell_id only — there is no agent_id field. When a session or agent finishes, its background shells continue running until the OC process exits or a kill_shell call is made.

## Parity reference
- CC: tasks/LocalShellTask/killShellTasks.ts:53-72 killShellTasksForAgent
- OC: missing — BackgroundShellManager has no agent_id field, no per-agent kill

## Acceptance
- BackgroundShell struct gets optional agent_id field
- On agent exit, all shells owned by that agent_id are terminated via terminate_process_tree
- Surfaces as cleanup hook in the session/agent lifecycle
